channel.title: Comments for page "Operation Galahad"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Operation Galahad"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this - an article devoted to pure collaborative worldbuilding, while controversial, is something I personally find interesting. But, as an avid world builder, I have some criticism to give.

+ Show
- hide

The Chitin Sea
Source of the River Tock… Immeasurably large biomass

This breaks one of the most important rules of worldbuilding 'rivers don't flow upwards.' As it is never specified that the river tock flows in reverse, or that the Chitan is built on higher ground than the rest of corbenic, so I'm gonna say that this river doesn't really make sense. It would probably be better to change this to something like:

The Chitin Sea
Found by following the Right Moon from the Great Desert, Valley of the Striders or following the River Tock to its mouth. Immeasurably large biomass…

Furthermore:

which is above the cloud layer and has not yet been observed, is said to contain the castle of SCP-PC-007

Is this cloud layer constant? If so, that should probably be specified.

Otherwise, I would love to see more of the culture of Corbenics inhabitants. At the moment we have some information on the structure of corbenic, but I'd love to see how the citizens of corbenic act, maybe in the form of 'essays' (maybe similar to those in SCP-4935 and SCP-5005) by project Galahad members based on testimonies from the Omega-16 group. But that's just my opinion. +1 anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This hasn't been added to in a minute, so I think I will take a stab at adding some stuff. My first idea is some kind of opening into 5800, although I don't know if the Foundation will actually know what the light pouring out of whatever land mark it is will be

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This been in my head for too long… Can I add an entity that manipulates the trees in the Southern part of the forest?

Edit: Added SCP-PC-013, "The Wood Baron" and the transcript.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've created a page for this for the SCP Explained wiki: http://scpexplained.wikidot.com/operation-galahad

item.title: New Stuff!
item.content:encoded: I added the Greenleaf Woods, SCP-PC-012, and the accompanying transcript.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i like em

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually would have been interested in the Santa bit, even if it was rewritten to not explicitly be Santa.

The entire thing just seems so relentlessly grimdark to the point of absurdity. This feels like something that would have come out of the first generation of SCPs, when grimdark was just about all anyone wrote. It just feels…anachronistic. And not in a great way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it's 3000.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've added The Croaking Cave, the Coldbound Hare, the Frogs that Spit Winter, and the Flame Bearer, and the Omega-16 log associated with them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: stuff in the afterlife

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ideas for what?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: can i just list some ideas?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is the eel a reference to another skip?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added some description of what's under the Chitin Sea. I'm really interested in expanding this world a little more- both this hub and the mainlist article toe a really nice line between showing things and leaving big gaps in the lore that make everything seem vaster and spookier. Hoping to add more questions than we answer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Removed the Santa bit. Doesn't fit with the tone even slightly. Sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's a cool core idea here, with parts of the story making things problematic. I feel like the story of the rebellious doctor suffers from too much information. Why not say something like: Phone contact became impossible, weird anomalous stuff that hints at attempts to communicate, then say her motive for this and the source of her apparent anomalous capabilities is unknown. The events gets worse as she becomes more desperate, but no one can tell what she wants. NOW it makes sense to send a team in.

The amoebic sea made me think of the Far Realm from D&D, a place of insanity outside reality that human brains aren't designed to process. Maybe something like people finding whatever they expect to see, with more lustful types ending up in the orgy spot, partiers in the marble hall, etc. Someone who is able to balance their baser desires with wisdom might move through realities as their perspectives change and ascend to true enlightenment after hundreds or thousands of years.

This is sort of a dump of my thoughts, not sure how I'll organize any of it, but I'm hoping to reignite interest in this cause I think the basic idea is sound, and could be great if properly developed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So I kinda liked this story, with the blatantly evil, grudge-holding Foundation and all, but… what the hell is the sudden random Santa thing?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added a Santa that gives out giant killer spiders for Christmas.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm, you could always add a generic cliched dark shadow that just… kills stuff and keeps them dead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In light of that change, I've decided to upvote this. I still have a few issues with it, but the core concept is unique and interesting enough to outweigh the nitpicks. Removing the 2718 reference was just enough to tip the scales in this page's favor.

2922 itself is still a no vote for me. On top of all my other problems with it, the concept just seems like it's better suited for a tale or a collaborative project like this than a mainlist SCP article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: you're speakin' my language >:3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting… I feel like it'd be a welcome addition to Corbenic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You raised a good point, so I've gone ahead and cut out all mentions of SCP-2718 in both this article and 2922.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You're absolutely right, the SCP wiki is full of conflicting canons and it already has several articles that address the afterlife. That makes me wonder all the more why you felt the need to reconcile 2922 with 2718. It's not like 2718 is part of the site's official canon and needs to be explained in the context of Corbenic for 2922 to work. It could just be ignored, with readers assuming that 2718 and 2922 are part of separate canons or deciding for themselves how the two can co-exist. You didn't explain how Giraffe Hell or Ice Cream Hell or Limb-o fit into Corbenic, so why bother explaining how 2718 fits? It just diminishes the horror of 2718 (making it into a mere "status effect", as you put it), and it also gives 2922 the feeling of a fix fic (http://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Main/FixFic).

I completely agree with you about thematic intention, but again, that just makes me more baffled about why you decided to weld together two SCPs with such dramatically different tones and themes. I actually prefer 2922's concept to 2718's, but tying the two together just makes both weaker.

item.title: Re: For collab peoples:
item.content:encoded: If I were you, I would make a more detailed map, but leave areas intentionally blank, for example if an object is expected to be in an area, but it hasn't been visited, then leave its map region blank,and sweet it is expected to be in that region

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd like to take this opportunity to formally address the canonical conflicts between 2922 and 2718, so thanks for providing me a chance.

First off, SCP-2718 is not the first article here to address an afterlife. We've got ice cream hell and giraffe hell, for example. The SCP wiki is full of conflicting canons. SCP-2718 is a really scary article, and I like what it's done. I've had literal nightmares about SCP-2718. The other night, even, I dreamed I got a SCP-2922 call from an MTF agent panicking and wondering why he was still in his body1. But "scarier" does not necessarily equal "more canonical."

I myself had doubts about SCP-2718 conflicting with SCP-2922, or reducing an established article to little more than a status effect in Corbenic (in this particular canon, anyway). I contacted user Michael Atreus, who made SCP-2718, about this, just so I could be sure that I wasn't disrespecting his intellectual property. Thankfully, he was very polite with me and also said that the canon on this site is anything but rigid.

I guess it's more of a matter of thematic intention. In my writing, I prefer to establish a world where there's always some hope of things getting better. Michael Atreus was going for pure hopeless terror. Neither style is superior to the other, they're just different.

Footnotes
1. (It was really cool, and a lot better than that dream I had about organizing and spearheading a national labor union for strippers so that I could go to various clubs and audit them for adherence to regs)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the notes regarding dialogue. PC-S12-1 has been changed accordingly. I understand that it might not be professional that S12-06 is "drunk-dialing" Henderson with SCP-2922, but bear in mind that she was just dragged into an eternal drunk blood orgy. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Go ahead. Feel free to upload without my permission.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
WhispersInTheDark more or less articulated my problems with both this and 2922. I thought it was rather dumb how the Foundation treated Spiegel especially when there were easier, more efficacious options available.

Unlike him, I greatly enjoyed 2718 and this little canon you're establishing does not feel like it does it justice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just read this and the associated SCP article, and while I'm intrigued by the core concept, I have a few problems with it.

First, the Foundation's response to Janet's initial request - which was a fairly reasonable one, in my opinion - was just outright bone-headed. I can understand why they'd refuse to agree to her demands, although even that's pushing it a little. (This woman has verifiable knowledge about the nature of the afterlife, you're not willing to bend the rules and break protocol for her, even just a little? For Christ's sake, the Foundation has amnestic drugs, couldn't they just give Janet her one phone call and then erase Herman's memory afterwards?) But randomly murdering her husband, just for spite and/or to prove a point about how they won't be blackmailed? That's just idiotic over-the-top cruelty. The Foundation is supposed to be cold, not cruel, but in that article they come across as not only excessively cruel but stupidly so.

Second, what exactly is the Foundation trying to accomplish with Operation Galahad? The main objective of the Grail Knights is apparently to track down Janet, but to what end? They know there's no way to bring her or the Grail Knights back. Given the nature of Corbenic, it's unlikely they'd be able to contain her in any meaningful way or force any information out of her. And thanks to their own stupidity (which the Grail Knights can't even address, since they're not aware of what happened), it's not likely she'd share information with them willingly. The document repeatedly stresses that this mission is crucial to the safety of the Foundation and humanity as a whole, but I really don't get the feeling that's actually the case, since it doesn't explain or even hint at how. Maybe the Project Corbenic directors are just lying, but considering the Grail Knights are secretly being killed for the sake of this expedition, I'd think the Foundation at least believes what they're doing is important. They wouldn't throw away that much valuable manpower for nothing.

Third, the references to SCP-2718 really rub me the wrong way. I hated that particular article, for reasons I've extensively detailed on the discussion page for it, so I don't care for how this article effectively canonizes it. But even if I did like it, I wouldn't be any happier about its inclusion here, since the whole concept of Corbenic massively contradicts 2718's portrayal of the afterlife. Your effort to reconcile the two feels extremely forced, and dramatically undercuts the intended horror of 2718. It's a cross-reference that not only doesn't add anything to either article (which is generally frowned upon in itself) but actively takes away from both. I was willing to look past the reference in SCP-2922, since it wasn't explicitly about SCP-2718 and at least theoretically could've been referring to something else, but this article makes the connection explicit by referencing 2718 directly, and that's hard for me to abide.

No vote for now, though that may change depending on how this project develops.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
More ways in which a human can be permanently trapped in an eternal nasty situation for all eternity, or the omnipresent healing factor could be negated

I wonder how many people are on the bottom of that river…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

your hand-to-hand combat abilities, skills of judgment, and stamina are all exceptional,

These can't possibly be the main criteria for doing anything Foundation related. I don't care if earth weapons are available or not.

I am happy to note that none of you have read the documentation for SCP-2922. Hopefully, I will not have to stress the importance of not breaking into a document long since labeled for Level 5 clearance.

So why even bring it up? "You know that thing you haven't read? Yeah, don't read it."

I imagine if these people are really such high level operatives they shouldn't need to be super double reminded not to look at documents they're not cleared for.

and will possess levels of energy that render rest unnecessary. Injuries are also prone to regenerate.

Oh so… why does stamina matter again?

Corbenic is an extradimensional realm of undefined volume.

"size" maybe

Gravity, air, and temperature readings are all similar to that of Earth.

What the fuck is an "air reading"?

The sky maintains a pea-green color. Weather is generally unchanging, temperature is usually breezy cold.

"Dark" green. Okay, and when it's not like that it's… stormy? Typhoons? Radiation storms? Important info, that.

There are no day or night cycles

Singular.

a mountain range known as the Valley of the Striders.

Known by whom?

through the desert for as long as possible

That's descriptive.

dangerous native species of primate - the Striders, its namesake

Calling Striders primates seems to be highly presumptuous at best.

believed to be over 30 km in height.

The Chitin Sea

Seriously, who's naming this shit?

Betrayed the Foundation when we refused to meet special demands regarding her husband.

This is more of an SCP-2922 related complaint, but I really hate this whole story/character arc.

Teleportation to an unknown area, and

Wouldn't they just be disappearing from existence from our perspective?

to duty after defecation.

Excretion

Damn it, she was only 23.

Ahahaha what? Who the fuck cares how old the Agent was, this is the Foundation we're talking about.

As a side note, current pet peeves: Radio communication that doesn't use proper pro words. Also, wait, they can bring radios but not guns? What?

A bunch of humans in royal clothes piled on her like wild animals and dragged her kicking and screaming into the Marble Hall, tearing her apart with their bare hands.

Who talks like this?

Your fears are irrelevant.

There's literally no possible way to say this sentence and not sound like an evil overlord.

I'm drunk, you moron.

Yeah we're stuck in an alternate dimension with an important mission, but I guess there's time to get drunk and rant at our superior officer.

02 can come by too if he wants. It was real scary at first. They were trying to rip my skin open and use it as a hat. But then I saw that it happens to everyone here. The torturers and the tortured switch places all the time. Everyone kills and eats and fucks each other, and it's all one big joke, and I'm dying laughing! This is frickin' amazing.

Seriously, all your dialogue needs rewriting to sound like real people. Also, they're willing to say asshole and dick, but not fucking?

This is basically just worldbuilding notes posted on to the main site. It's interesting worldbuilding, but there's no story to make them worth posting. Two paragraphs of cheesy dialogue don't count.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, I'm interested in writing a transcript involving a group of MTF Ω-16 agents, and their first couple of days in Corbenic. Once I am finished with it, is it ok if I PM you to make sure the story fits within this collab?

item.title: For collab peoples:
item.content:encoded:
Here is a partial map of Corbenic that I have constructed. Bear in mind that some of the areas haven't been described yet or placed yet. I leave those up to anyone who wishes to have input.

As for the area outside the boundaries of Corbenic, I haven't decided that either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm attempting to make a map of Corbenic's main understood area, which will be more full as we go along, naturally

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mind reminding me of this SCP's number?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm thinking he/she hangs out in this place between reincarnations, and forms cults here too, being the only person who can escape and return to the living world.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As this stands I'm afraid I have to downvote, while I like the idea it feels a bit bare bones right now.

I might very well change my vote if people are going to add to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Adding a new area and transcript relating to the Marble Hall.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you like!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can I tie the reincarnating cult leader SCP into this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Vis à vis the tagging situation: you can have this be a tale, and not a supplement, or you can have this be a supplement, but not a tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-PC-003 is one of the few entities in Corbenic known to eat. Striders have been known to collect wayward human subjects in stone baskets to feed SCP-PC-007. If captured for this purpose, it is crucial to cooperate and refrain from resistance.

I think this should be SCP-PC-007, since the Queen eats people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm… I can't really weigh in on this until there are Tales to show the flow of things. The concept of an MTF cruising the afterlives is interesting indeed, but one must lead by example (as a wise man once said).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm…

Check your PMs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes, yes you can.

Corbenic is a huge place meant to be a hub to all the possible afterlives. You end up in the desert when you die, and you make your journey from there, utilizing skills you've learned throughout your life. Your final destination could be good or bad, and it all depends on your choices.

What it needs is -
- More places to go
- More dangers
- More ways in which a human can be permanently trapped in an eternal nasty situation for all eternity, or the omnipresent healing factor could be negated
- More races and creatures and monsters

MTF Omega-16 report transcripts would also be welcome, perhaps a new tab for that is in order. Keep in mind, the majority of them don't know they're not coming back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can I ask for some elaboration on what can/should be added?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Collaboration is open. Add what you will, just log it here.
